# Preparing session


::: {.cell}

:::


## Explore data - Numerically


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cor_insect_vs_noise<-cor(urb_fledg$insect_abundance_g_m_2, urb_fledg$environmental_noise_dB)
#strong negative correlation
```
:::


We have a negative correlation of -0.78 which means that there is an interaction between the environmental noise and insect abundance.

## Explore data - Graphically


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggarrange(
  ggplot(urb_fledg, aes(x=environmental_noise_dB, y=number_of_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method="lm")+
    xlab("Enrironmental noise (dB)")+
    ylab("Number of fledglings")
  ,
  
  ggplot(urb_fledg, aes(x=insect_abundance_g_m_2, y=number_of_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method="lm")+
    xlab("Insect abundance (g/m^2)")+
    ylab("Number of fledglings"),
  common.legend = TRUE, labels=c("a)", "b)")
  
)

  ggplot(urb_fledg, aes(x=insect_abundance_g_m_2, y=environmental_noise_dB))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method="lm")+
    xlab("Insect abundance (g/m^2)")+
    ylab("noise")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Exploratory plots; a) higher environmental noise (dB) is associated with fewer fledglings; b) higher insect abundance (g/m^2) is associated with a greater number of fledglings.](Analysis_urban_fledg_files/figure-html/fig-exp-graph-1.png){#fig-exp-graph-1 width=566.929133858268}
:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Exploratory plots; a) higher environmental noise (dB) is associated with fewer fledglings; b) higher insect abundance (g/m^2) is associated with a greater number of fledglings.](Analysis_urban_fledg_files/figure-html/fig-exp-graph-2.png){#fig-exp-graph-2 width=566.929133858268}
:::
:::


As we can see in the @fig-exp-graph we have a positive correlation between the number of fledglings and the insect abundance and a negative correlation to environmental noise.


# Fit the models

## Fit candidate models

::: {#tbl-candidate-models .cell tbl-cap='Candidate model comparisons'}

```{.r .cell-code}
model.00<-glm(number_of_fledglings~1, urb_fledg, family=poisson)
model.01<-glm(number_of_fledglings~insect_abundance_g_m_2, urb_fledg, family=poisson)
model.02<-glm(number_of_fledglings~insect_abundance_g_m_2+environmental_noise_dB, urb_fledg, family=poisson)
model.03<-glm(number_of_fledglings~insect_abundance_g_m_2*environmental_noise_dB, urb_fledg, family=poisson)

knitr::kable(compare_performance(model.00, model.01, model.02, model.03), digits=3)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


|Name     |Model |     AIC| AIC_wt|    AICc| AICc_wt|     BIC| BIC_wt| R2_Nagelkerke|  RMSE| Sigma| Score_log| Score_spherical|
|:--------|:-----|-------:|------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|------:|-------------:|-----:|-----:|---------:|---------------:|
|model.00 |glm   | 143.887|  0.004| 143.998|   0.005| 145.524|  0.012|         0.000| 1.275|     1|    -1.867|           0.160|
|model.01 |glm   | 133.798|  0.656| 134.141|   0.708| 137.073|  0.827|         0.932| 0.341|     1|    -1.708|           0.161|
|model.02 |glm   | 135.743|  0.248| 136.448|   0.223| 140.655|  0.138|         0.936| 0.330|     1|    -1.707|           0.161|
|model.03 |glm   | 137.729|  0.092| 138.941|   0.064| 144.279|  0.023|         0.937| 0.328|     1|    -1.707|           0.161|


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
check_model(model.02)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Diagnostic plots](Analysis_urban_fledg_files/figure-html/fig-diagnostic-model-1.png){#fig-diagnostic-model width=944.88188976378}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
#check overdispersion

check_overdispersion(model.02)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# Overdispersion test

       dispersion ratio = 0.029
  Pearson's Chi-Squared = 1.005
                p-value =     1
```


:::
:::




## Interpret the chosen model

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
coef_table<-round(summary(model.02)$coefficients,3)

r.2<-round(attributes(r.squaredLR(model.02))$adj.r.squared,3)

null_chi<-anova(model.00, model.02)
```
:::




::: {#tbl-glm .cell tbl-cap='Estimates, associated standard errors and statistical significance of the parameters estimated by a poisson GLM ($R^2$= 0.28 , $\chi^2_{( 2 , 35 )}$= 12.144 , $p$ = 0.002 )'}

```{.r .cell-code}
knitr::kable(coef_table)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


|                       | Estimate| Std. Error| z value| Pr(>|z|)|
|:----------------------|--------:|----------:|-------:|------------------:|
|(Intercept)            |    0.896|      1.417|   0.632|              0.527|
|insect_abundance_g_m_2 |    0.083|      0.041|   2.043|              0.041|
|environmental_noise_dB |   -0.004|      0.018|  -0.236|              0.814|


:::
:::



A poisson Generalized Linear Model (*R*^2^= 0.28 , $\chi^2_{( 2 , 35 )}$= 12.144 , *p*0.0023064





















::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(urb_fledg, aes(x=insect_abundance_g_m_2, y=environmental_noise_dB))+
  geom_point(aes(size=number_of_fledglings, color=number_of_fledglings), alpha=.7)
```
